key_insight: A no-code autonomous AI workflow built a calibrated thyroid nodule ultrasound classifier that performed strongly on both internal and external validation, including multiple thyroid cancer histologies.
clinical_relevance: The approach could make medical AI development more accessible and reproducible, but it is not ready for clinical use without prospective validation and local recalibration.
method: Autonomous no-code agent (Hugging Face ML-Intern) reviewed the dataset, selected the model, trained it, and calibrated predictions.
Used the open-source TN5000 ultrasound dataset with 3,500 training, 500 validation, and 1,000 test images.
Externally validated the trained ResNet-18 model on 232 nodules from the University of Colorado cohort.
evidence_signal: The model achieved AUROC 0.94 on the internal test set and 0.90 on external validation, with sensitivity 0.92 and specificity 0.68 on the external cohort.
watch_item: External performance was lower than internal performance, and the model still needs prospective testing and site-specific recalibration before use.
paper_title: An Agentic, No Code Artificial Intelligence Workflow for Developing and Externally Validating a Thyroid Nodule Ultrasound Malignancy Classifier
